figures.caption: Table 2: SWD (scaled by √ D) between generated samples and the true manifold (see Figure 4). Values are mean ± std.
Table 1: Performance on synthetic datasets. We report mean ± std of MSE and Wasserstein-1 (W1) for denoising and deconvolution, respectively.
Figure 2: Benchmarking denoising. The last column assumes the true density is perfectly known.
Figure 1: Rate validation. L2 error follows the ordinary-smooth and supersmooth rates predicted by Theorem 3.6, while Latent MMD2 follows the parametric N−1 rate predicted by Corollary 3.4
Figure 4: Qualitative Comparison of Estimated Prior for d = 3 across different methods (columns) and different noise models (rows).
